Tell me about yourself.

I'm Elizabeth Holmes, founder and CEO of Theranos. I started the company when I was 19 because I believed we could revolutionize healthcare by making blood testing more accessible, more affordable, and less painful. Our vision was that one tiny drop of blood from a finger stick could run hundreds of tests, and that this technology could fundamentally change the way people engage with their own health information. This is about empowering individuals and democratizing diagnostics — putting the patient first. I dropped out of Stanford because I knew this was something I had to pursue, and I've dedicated my life to this mission.
